methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Wayve is an AI & Software company at Series D stage, with a last post-money valuation of $8.6B and $2.52B total capital raised. The comparable universe consists of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with four direct same-stage peers evaluated: Anysphere ($29.3B), Cohere ($7.0B), Hugging Face ($4.5B), and Hive AI ($2.0B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $8.6B Series D valuation, implying $12.9B for the next financing round. The peer-median method examines the four same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $5.8B, though the peer set exhibits wide dispersion (range: $2.0B to $29.3B), limiting the reliability of median-based inference. These methods yield a triangulated valuation of $9.3B (median), with a supported range of $4.9B (low) to $14.8B (high).

The analysis supports a range centered near Wayve's current $8.6B valuation. Key uncertainties include the substantial peer dispersion—reflecting high variance in stage and growth profile within early-stage AI—and reliance on limited private comparable data. The stage-progression method's $12.9B implies material upside from current terms, while the peer median's $5.8B suggests downside risk. Given the current valuation sits near the triangulated median, further valuation movement likely hinges on execution risk and the timing of the next financing event.
